retry_reason: dev_kfree_skb_irq unconditionally releases the reference; no failure path exists, so retry is irrelevant.
contract_summary: This function is a batch-release (put) wrapper that empties an sk_buff_head queue by calling dev_kfree_skb_irq on each dequeued skb. It does not perform any atomic operations directly; it relies on the callee to decrement the reference count. The caller must ensure that the queue is valid and that no other context is using the skbs after this call (the function consumes them). After calling this function, the list will be empty. No return value; caller does not need to check anything. This is typically used in interrupt context (as the name _irq suggests).

judge.reason: The sole purpose is to deallocate all skbs in the queue by repeatedly calling dev_kfree_skb_irq, which is a put function on the skb's reference count. No other business logic exists.
